title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Kroll expresses concern to Miss Marple regarding the difficulty of verifying identities, noting that organized groups use forged or borrowed identity cards to commit fraud. He shares information about two nicknames, "Pip" and "Emma," who may be relatives of Mrs. Randall-Godley. Kroll hopes to obtain more information about them from Mrs. Goddard if she is able to speak. While Kroll is anxious about the potential danger to Letitia Blacklock, Cora Crofton asserts that she has also warned Miss Marple.

Later, during tea at the Blacklock residence, Miss Marple meets Letitia Blacklock for the first time. Miss Marple discusses home security and theft with the hostess and her guests, Mrs. Harmon and Miss Bonner. Miss Bonner mentions that Chief Inspector Corridick was acting strangely by inspecting a second door that would not open, noting that it appeared to have been oiled. Letitia Blacklock expresses annoyance at the discussion of the Inspector's experiments. 

The group discusses the recent robbery, during which a man reportedly tripped and shot himself. Patrick joins the conversation and helps demonstrate the location of the bullet holes in the room to Miss Marple. During the gathering, Miss Bonner expresses disapproval over a cigarette burn on a silver table, highlighting her intense attachment to Letitia’s possessions. Miss Marple concludes the conversation by mentioning her own fondness for keeping childhood photographs of her nieces and nephews.
